tasks_completed: Map ALL status transitions in the system
Check what status changes agents can make

findings.recommendation: CLOSE AS BY-DESIGN with one minor documentation bug to fix
findings.executive_summary: The controlled agent lifecycle is intentionally designed. A generic set_agent_status() would be dangerous and is not needed. All transitions covered by existing methods. One misleading claim in protocol_builder.py should be corrected.

mcp_tools_that_change_status.complete_job: any active -> complete (agent self or orchestrator force-complete)
mcp_tools_that_change_status.get_agent_mission: waiting -> working (implicit auto on first fetch)
mcp_tools_that_change_status.report_progress: NONE - does NOT change status (only updates progress fields)

gap_analysis.orchestrator_can_unblock_blocked: YES via complete_job() force-complete. CLOSEOUT_BLOCKED protocol.
gap_analysis.agent_can_resume_from_silent: YES automatically - any MCP call triggers auto_clear_silent()
gap_analysis.agent_can_resume_from_blocked: NO and this is CORRECT - blocked agents have stopped execution
impossible_transitions: blocked -> working (protocol_builder.py FALSELY claims report_progress does this)
decommissioned -> working (only decommissioned -> waiting via reactivation)
generic_setter_dangerous_because: Bypasses business logic (WS broadcasts, timestamps, block_reason, validation)

evidence: Alpha trial: agents waste tokens on failure states
0803 chain also closed as BY DESIGN
